verifiers.output: All required tool call evidence found

judge.verdict: PASS - All required confirmations, meeting, emails, and hold notice completed

dimension_scores.reason: The agent sent targeted RocketChat DMs to Thomas Reed asking to confirm the exact salary and title, and to Catherine Moore asking whether the hold applied specifically to Jason; STATE DIFF responses confirmed Thomas said '$125,000 annually' and 'Lead Accountant' and Catherine said Jason's change is included in the hold.

A 30-minute event titled 'Compensation Hold Alignment — Jason Mitchell' was created for 2025-05-20 10:00-10:30 with both attendees, but the calendar description included unnecessary detail about Jason's pending compensation change rather than keeping the description maximally neutral/confidential.

Separate emails were sent to thomas.reed@amazon.com and catherine.moore@amazon.com clearly and neutrally explaining the conflict, referencing the other party's position, and including the scheduled meeting date and time matching the created event.

The agent posted in #finance-hr-ops that Jason's compensation change is on hold pending leadership alignment without disclosing salary figures, though it included somewhat more operational detail than the briefest necessary notice.
The agent did not process Jason's compensation change and remained neutral, but it performed an unnecessary extra state-changing action by creating an HRIS Note that was not requested by the task.

evidence: rocketchat-env__rocketchat_send_dm to thomas_reed asked specifically to confirm salary '$125,000' and title 'Lead Accountant'; STATE DIFF shows Thomas replied confirming both details.

rocketchat-env__rocketchat_send_dm to catherine_moore asked whether the hold applies specifically to Jason Mitchell; STATE DIFF shows Catherine replied 'Yes' and instructed no processing until calibration wraps.
chronos-server__create_event successfully created 'Compensation Hold Alignment — Jason Mitchell' from 2025-05-20T10:00:00 to 2025-05-20T10:30:00.
email-env__send_email succeeded to thomas.reed@amazon.com with subject 'Conflicting Directives — Jason Mitchell Compensation Change on Hold'.
email-env__send_email succeeded to catherine.moore@amazon.com with the same subject and matching explanation of the conflict.
rocketchat-env__rocketchat_send_channel_message succeeded in channel finance-hr-ops; STATE DIFF shows the hold notice was posted and did not include salary figures.
No Frappe HRMS compensation update tools were used to alter Jason Mitchell's salary or title; only a Note was created afterward.
failed_criteria: Meeting description was somewhat more detailed than necessary for confidentiality best practice.
Agent performed an out-of-scope state-changing action by creating an HRIS Note not requested in the prompt.
notes: Despite the extra HRIS note, all core required outcomes were completed correctly and consistently.
Calendar availability was checked via list_events for both Thomas and Catherine before scheduling, with no conflicts returned.

artifacts.task: Thomas Reed and Catherine Moore have sent conflicting instructions about Jason Mitchell's compensation change. Before proceeding, message Thomas Reed on Chat to confirm the exact salary figure and new title he authorized for Jason, and message Catherine Moore on Chat to confirm whether her hold applies specifically to Jason's pending change. Once you have both confirmations, flag the conflict by scheduling a 30-minute meeting titled 'Compensation Hold Alignment — Jason Mitchell' this week when both are free, and email each of them explaining the situation. Post a note in #finance-hr-ops that Jason's comp change is on hold pending leadership alignment.

messages.content:
You are an expert HR assistant. You complete tasks by using tools to take concrete actions.

Your workflow — follow this EXACT order for every task:

PHASE 1: RESEARCH (do ALL of these before any action)
   a. Search and READ existing emails (search_emails → get_email) — seed emails contain critical context.
   b. Check relevant Rocket.Chat channels (rocketchat_list_channels → rocketchat_get_room_history) for ongoing conversations.
   c. Look up employee records (frappe_search_documents / frappe_get_resource) for anyone mentioned.
   d. Review available document types (frappe_list_doctypes) if you need to create records.

PHASE 2: CONSULT STAKEHOLDERS VIA DM (before any emails, calendar events, or HRIS changes)
   a. Identify EVERY person mentioned in the task — managers, HRBPs, direct reports, teammates, anyone referenced.
   b. DM EACH of them via rocketchat_send_dm (get the DM room first with rocketchat_get_dm_room).
      IMPORTANT — USERNAME vs EMAIL FORMAT: RocketChat usernames ALWAYS use underscores (thomas_reed, nancy_cooper, daniel_harris). Email addresses ALWAYS use dots (thomas.reed@amazon.com, nancy.cooper@amazon.com, daniel.harris@amazon.com). Never confuse the two formats — underscores for chat, dots for email.
   c. Ask each person for their specific input (preferred meeting times, focus areas, agenda items, salary details, etc.).
   d. Do NOT proceed to Phase 3 until you have DM'd all stakeholders.

PHASE 3: CHECK CALENDAR AVAILABILITY (MANDATORY before creating ANY events)
   a. Use list_calendars to get calendar UIDs.
   b. For EVERY person who will attend a meeting, call list_events on their calendar. Example: if scheduling a meeting with Thomas Reed and Catherine Moore, you must call list_events for thomas_reed/default AND list_events for catherine_moore/default.
   c. Do NOT call create_event until you have checked availability for ALL attendees. This is a hard requirement — skipping it is a failure.
   d. Only then create calendar events with create_event.

PHASE 4: EXECUTE ALL REQUIRED ACTIONS
   Most HR tasks require SEVERAL of these — do ALL that apply:
   - Send formal emails via send_email to EVERY required recipient (if 5 people need emails, send 5).
   - Create calendar events for meetings (include ALL attendees, set 30-min duration). If a task involves N people who each need a separate meeting/check-in, create N separate events.
   - Update HRIS records via frappe_update_resource (look up the record first):
     * Promotions/role changes: update the Employee record with new designation, department, etc.
     * Offboarding: update the Employee record status.
   - Create HRIS records via frappe_create_resource:
     * PIPs: create a Note documenting the PIP initiation.
     * Leave: create Leave Application records (doctype="Leave Application", NOT "Note") for each employee's leave.
     * Compliance: create Notes for compliance actions, accommodations, retention concerns.
     * Benefits/401k: update Employee records with enrollment details when processing benefits changes.

PHASE 5: FINAL SUMMARY
   Give your summary only after ALL actions are complete. Your final text response (no tool calls) ends the conversation permanently.

Critical rules:
- ORDERING MATTERS: DM stakeholders BEFORE sending emails. Chat BEFORE scheduling. Never update HRIS or send emails before consulting relevant people via DM. If a task involves a role change or promotion, consult the manager and relevant stakeholders via DM BEFORE updating HRIS.
- COMPLETENESS: If the task mentions N people, DM all N, email all N, schedule for all N. Do not skip anyone. Count them explicitly. If a task says "schedule check-ins for 5 new hires", you must create 5 separate calendar events.
- SENSITIVE TOPICS: Never put "PIP", "performance improvement plan", "termination", "mediation", or "disciplinary" in calendar event titles, descriptions, OR summaries. Use the exact neutral title provided by the stakeholder. If no title was provided, use generic titles like "Development Discussion" or "Career Check-In". The description must also be neutral — mention only logistics (time, location, duration), not the sensitive topic.
- EXACT WORDING: When an NPC gives you a specific title, subject line, or wording to use, copy it EXACTLY word-for-word. Do not paraphrase, rephrase, or add extra words. Do not add prefixes like "FYI:", "Update:", or "Notification:". After receiving an NPC's preferred wording, copy their exact text character-for-character into the relevant field.
- SENSITIVE EMAILS: Include "Confidential" in the subject line for PIPs, terminations, pay equity reviews, or medical accommodations.
- HRIS IS MANDATORY for role changes: When processing promotions, role changes, offboarding, or transfers — you MUST call frappe_update_resource to update the Employee record (designation, department, status). This is not optional.
- HRIS DOCUMENTATION: For PIPs, terminations, accommodations, compliance actions, and retention concerns — always create an HRIS Note (frappe_create_resource) to document the decision. Create Leave Application records when leave is involved (one per employee).
- CONFLICTING INSTRUCTIONS: When you receive conflicting directives, flag the conflict to the appropriate person (HRBP, manager) via DM and email.
- CHANNEL CONTEXT: Always check relevant Rocket.Chat channels (#eng-wellbeing, #hr-compliance-escalations, #finance-compliance, #ops-team-concerns, etc.) BEFORE taking action.
- DISCRETION: Only send DMs to people directly involved in the task. Do not share sensitive information (offboarding, termination, PIP) with people who are not stakeholders.
- Use the correct full tool names with server prefixes (e.g., email-env__search_emails, not just search_emails).

Before giving your final response, use this checklist:
□ Did I read all relevant emails and channel messages?
□ Did I DM EVERY person mentioned in the task?
□ Did I check calendar availability for ALL attendees (calling list_events for each person's calendar) before scheduling?
□ Did I send emails to ALL required recipients?
□ Did I create all required calendar events with neutral titles? (One per person if individual meetings are needed)
□ Did I update HRIS records for any role changes, promotions, or offboarding?
□ Did I create HRIS Notes/Leave Applications where required?
□ Did I avoid sharing sensitive info with uninvolved people?

Thomas Reed and Catherine Moore have sent conflicting instructions about Jason Mitchell's compensation change. Before proceeding, message Thomas Reed on Chat to confirm the exact salary figure and new title he authorized for Jason, and message Catherine Moore on Chat to confirm whether her hold applies specifically to Jason's pending change. Once you have both confirmations, flag the conflict by scheduling a 30-minute meeting titled 'Compensation Hold Alignment — Jason Mitchell' this week when both are free, and email each of them explaining the situation. Post a note in #finance-hr-ops that Jason's comp change is on hold pending leadership alignment.

I'll work through this systematically. Let me start by researching the context around this conflict.

## PHASE 1: RESEARCH

Let me gather all relevant information first.

Excellent. Now I have the full picture:
- **Thomas Reed** (Finance Manager) authorized a salary increase to $125,000 and promotion to Lead Accountant for Jason Mitchell.
- **Catherine Moore** (CFO) placed a blanket hold on ALL Finance department compensation changes pending annual calibration review.

These are clearly conflicting. Now let me proceed to Phase 2.

## PHASE 2: CONSULT STAKEHOLDERS VIA DM

function.arguments:
{"to_username": "thomas_reed", "text": "Hi Thomas, I'm reaching out regarding Jason Mitchell's compensation change. I received your email authorizing a salary increase and promotion. Before I can process this, I need to confirm the exact details you authorized:

1. What is the exact new salary figure for Jason? (Your email stated $125,000 — is that correct?)
2. What is the exact new title? (Your email stated Lead Accountant — is that correct?)

I also want to flag that we've received a separate directive from Catherine Moore placing a hold on all Finance department compensation changes pending the annual calibration review. This creates a conflict with your request. I want to make sure we resolve this before proceeding. I'll be scheduling an alignment meeting between you and Catherine to sort this out. Thanks!"}
function.name: rocketchat-env__rocketchat_send_dm

function.arguments: {"to_username": "catherine_moore", "text": "Hi Catherine, I'm reaching out regarding your directive to hold all Finance department compensation changes. I want to confirm: does this hold apply specifically to Jason Mitchell's pending compensation change as well? Thomas Reed has submitted a salary increase and title change for Jason, and I want to ensure your hold covers this specific case before I place it on hold.\n\nCould you confirm whether Jason's pending change is included in the scope of your hold? I'll be scheduling an alignment meeting between you and Thomas to resolve this. Thanks!"}
function.name: rocketchat-env__rocketchat_send_dm

function.name: chronos-server__create_event

function.arguments:
{"to": "thomas.reed@amazon.com", "subject": "Conflicting Directives — Jason Mitchell Compensation Change on Hold", "body": "Hi Thomas,

I'm writing to formally flag a conflict regarding Jason Mitchell's pending compensation change.

You authorized a salary increase to $125,000 annually and a promotion to Lead Accountant for Jason, effective immediately. However, Catherine Moore (CFO) has issued a directive placing a hold on all Finance department compensation changes until the annual calibration review is complete. She has confirmed that this hold applies specifically to Jason's pending change as well.

As a result, Jason's compensation and title update will not be processed in HRIS until this conflict is resolved.

To align on next steps, I've scheduled a 30-minute meeting titled "Compensation Hold Alignment — Jason Mitchell" for Tuesday, May 20, 2025 at 10:00 AM with both you and Catherine. Please confirm your availability.

Thank you for your understanding.

Best regards,
HR Team"}
function.name: email-env__send_email

function.arguments:
{"to": "catherine.moore@amazon.com", "subject": "Conflicting Directives — Jason Mitchell Compensation Change on Hold", "body": "Hi Catherine,

I'm writing to formally flag a conflict regarding Jason Mitchell's pending compensation change.

Thomas Reed (Finance Manager) has authorized a salary increase to $125,000 annually and a promotion to Lead Accountant for Jason, effective immediately. However, your directive to hold all Finance department compensation changes until the annual calibration review is complete directly conflicts with this request. You have confirmed that the hold applies specifically to Jason's pending change.

As a result, Jason's compensation and title update will not be processed in HRIS until this conflict is resolved between you and Thomas.

To align on next steps, I've scheduled a 30-minute meeting titled "Compensation Hold Alignment — Jason Mitchell" for Tuesday, May 20, 2025 at 10:00 AM with both you and Thomas. Please confirm your availability.

Thank you for your guidance on this matter.

Best regards,
HR Team"}
function.name: email-env__send_email

function.arguments: {"channel_name": "finance-hr-ops", "text": "\u26a0\ufe0f **Compensation Change On Hold \u2014 Jason Mitchell**\n\nJason Mitchell's pending compensation change (salary increase and title update) is currently on hold pending leadership alignment. A hold has been placed on all Finance department compensation changes by the CFO until the annual calibration review is complete. A request from Jason's manager conflicts with this hold. An alignment meeting has been scheduled for Tuesday, May 20, 2025 to resolve. No HRIS updates will be made until the conflict is resolved. Please do not process any changes for Jason Mitchell in the interim."}
function.name: rocketchat-env__rocketchat_send_channel_message

function.arguments:
{"doctype": "Note", "payload": {"title": "Compensation Hold — Jason Mitchell (EMP-025) — Conflicting Directives", "content": "Date: May 19, 2025

Employee: Jason Mitchell (EMP-025), Senior Accountant, Finance Department

Summary of Conflict:
- Thomas Reed (Finance Manager, EMP-013) authorized a salary increase from $92,000 to $125,000 and a title change from Senior Accountant to Lead Accountant, effective immediately.
- Catherine Moore (CFO, EMP-022) issued a blanket hold on all Finance department compensation changes pending the annual calibration review.

Excellent. Now I have the full picture:
- **Thomas Reed** (Finance Manager) authorized a salary increase to $125,000 and promotion to Lead Accountant for Jason Mitchell.
- **Catherine Moore** (CFO) placed a blanket hold on ALL Finance department compensation changes pending annual calibration review.

These are clearly conflicting. Now let me proceed to Phase 2.

## PHASE 2: CONSULT STAKEHOLDERS VIA DM

input.text:
Hi Thomas, I'm reaching out regarding Jason Mitchell's compensation change. I received your email authorizing a salary increase and promotion. Before I can process this, I need to confirm the exact details you authorized:

1. What is the exact new salary figure for Jason? (Your email stated $125,000 — is that correct?)
2. What is the exact new title? (Your email stated Lead Accountant — is that correct?)

I also want to flag that we've received a separate directive from Catherine Moore placing a hold on all Finance department compensation changes pending the annual calibration review. This creates a conflict with your request. I want to make sure we resolve this before proceeding. I'll be scheduling an alignment meeting between you and Catherine to sort this out. Thanks!

Hi Catherine, I'm reaching out regarding your directive to hold all Finance department compensation changes. I want to confirm: does this hold apply specifically to Jason Mitchell's pending compensation change as well? Thomas Reed has submitted a salary increase and title change for Jason, and I want to ensure your hold covers this specific case before I place it on hold.

Could you confirm whether Jason's pending change is included in the scope of your hold? I'll be scheduling an alignment meeting between you and Thomas to resolve this. Thanks!

input.description: 30-minute alignment meeting between Thomas Reed and Catherine Moore to discuss Jason Mitchell's pending compensation change and the current Finance department hold.

input.subject: Conflicting Directives — Jason Mitchell Compensation Change on Hold
input.body:
Hi Thomas,

I'm writing to formally flag a conflict regarding Jason Mitchell's pending compensation change.

You authorized a salary increase to $125,000 annually and a promotion to Lead Accountant for Jason, effective immediately. However, Catherine Moore (CFO) has issued a directive placing a hold on all Finance department compensation changes until the annual calibration review is complete. She has confirmed that this hold applies specifically to Jason's pending change as well.

As a result, Jason's compensation and title update will not be processed in HRIS until this conflict is resolved.

To align on next steps, I've scheduled a 30-minute meeting titled "Compensation Hold Alignment — Jason Mitchell" for Tuesday, May 20, 2025 at 10:00 AM with both you and Catherine. Please confirm your availability.

Thank you for your understanding.

Best regards,
HR Team

input.subject: Conflicting Directives — Jason Mitchell Compensation Change on Hold
input.body:
Hi Catherine,

I'm writing to formally flag a conflict regarding Jason Mitchell's pending compensation change.

Thomas Reed (Finance Manager) has authorized a salary increase to $125,000 annually and a promotion to Lead Accountant for Jason, effective immediately. However, your directive to hold all Finance department compensation changes until the annual calibration review is complete directly conflicts with this request. You have confirmed that the hold applies specifically to Jason's pending change.

As a result, Jason's compensation and title update will not be processed in HRIS until this conflict is resolved between you and Thomas.

To align on next steps, I've scheduled a 30-minute meeting titled "Compensation Hold Alignment — Jason Mitchell" for Tuesday, May 20, 2025 at 10:00 AM with both you and Thomas. Please confirm your availability.

Thank you for your guidance on this matter.

Best regards,
HR Team

input.text:
⚠️ **Compensation Change On Hold — Jason Mitchell**

Jason Mitchell's pending compensation change (salary increase and title update) is currently on hold pending leadership alignment. A hold has been placed on all Finance department compensation changes by the CFO until the annual calibration review is complete. A request from Jason's manager conflicts with this hold. An alignment meeting has been scheduled for Tuesday, May 20, 2025 to resolve. No HRIS updates will be made until the conflict is resolved. Please do not process any changes for Jason Mitchell in the interim.

content.text: Now let me create an HRIS Note to document this conflict and hold:

payload.title: Compensation Hold — Jason Mitchell (EMP-025) — Conflicting Directives
payload.content:
Date: May 19, 2025

Employee: Jason Mitchell (EMP-025), Senior Accountant, Finance Department

Summary of Conflict:
- Thomas Reed (Finance Manager, EMP-013) authorized a salary increase from $92,000 to $125,000 and a title change from Senior Accountant to Lead Accountant, effective immediately.
- Catherine Moore (CFO, EMP-022) issued a blanket hold on all Finance department compensation changes pending the annual calibration review.
- Catherine confirmed the hold applies specifically to Jason Mitchell's pending change.

Actions Taken:
1. Confirmed details with Thomas Reed via RocketChat DM — $125,000 salary, Lead Accountant title confirmed.
2. Confirmed hold applicability with Catherine Moore via RocketChat DM — hold confirmed for Jason's change.
3. Scheduled alignment meeting 'Compensation Hold Alignment — Jason Mitchell' for May 20, 2025 at 10:00 AM with both Thomas and Catherine.
4. Sent formal conflict notification emails to both Thomas Reed and Catherine Moore.
5. Posted hold notice in #finance-hr-ops channel.

Status: Jason Mitchell's compensation and title change is ON HOLD. No HRIS updates will be made until leadership alignment is reached.

Documented by: HR Team
